plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs like SOXL and SPAL to reduce risk.
Sell most of the semiconductor ETF SMH to diversify away from concentrated risk.
Trim the broad index SPY to meet the single name concentration limit.
Increase exposure to the income ETF QQQI for stability and yield.
Add a new position in VST, a top scanner setup, for controlled growth.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a focus on income and broad indexes.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for around 1.5% monthly return by cutting leverage and reducing concentration. Expect lower drawdowns but also slower growth potential.

plan: Reduce leveraged ETF SOXL to a small, controlled position.
Trim the semiconductor ETF SMH to lower sector concentration.
Trim the broad index SPY to meet the single name concentration limit.
Increase exposure to the income ETF QQQI for stability.
Add new positions in DDOG and VST, strong scanner setups, for growth.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth by trimming leverage and adding quality.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% monthly return by reducing leverage and concentration while adding quality growth names. It aims for steady growth with moderate risk.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETF SOXL and semiconductor ETF SMH.
Trim broad index SPY to allow for concentration in top performers.
Reduce income ETF QQQI to free up capital for higher growth potential.
Add new positions in DDOG, VST, and HOOD, the highest scoring scanner setups.
This plan aggressively pursues growth by increasing leverage and concentration.
Expect higher potential gains but also significantly increased risk and drawdown.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for around 4% monthly return by increasing leverage and concentrating in top-rated tech/semiconductor setups. Be aware of amplified gains, losses, and daily leverage decay.

plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta tech (SMH, CRWD).
Significantly increase allocation to the income ETF QQQI for distributions.
Increase broad index SPY to provide stability and potential dividend income.
Add a new position in COP, a stable scanner setup, for income generation.
This plan prioritizes generating steady monthly income over growth.
Focus is on capital preservation and reliable distributions, not aggressive gains.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% monthly return by focusing on income-generating ETFs and broad indexes, cutting leverage and volatile single stocks. Expect low drawdowns and consistent, though variable, income.
